items.title: Chollet: current AI systems lack metacognition — they cannot accurately model their own capabilities
items.summary: François Chollet argues that LLMs fundamentally lack metacognition: they cannot accurately assess what they know or don't know, producing confident errors and unreliable self-evaluation. He frames this as a key gap separating current systems from robust general reasoning, with direct implications for building reliable agentic pipelines.

items.title: QIMMA: A Quality-First Arabic LLM Leaderboard
items.summary: Hugging Face and TII launch QIMMA, a quality-first leaderboard for Arabic LLMs covering dialect, culture, and reasoning benchmarks. Fills a critical gap in non-English model evaluation, relevant for any multilingual or regional AI deployment work.

items.title: LeCun: AI existential risk framing is wrong — misuse and power concentration are the real dangers
items.summary: Yann LeCun pushes back on p(doom) framing, arguing the real AI risk is not autonomous superintelligence but concentration of AI power in a few companies and misuse by state or corporate actors. He calls for open-source AI development and international governance structures as the appropriate countermeasures.

items.title: OpenAI Images v2 rolling out to all ChatGPT accounts
items.summary: OpenAI Images v2, an upgraded image generation capability, is now available across all ChatGPT tiers. Confirmed via Logan Kilpatrick on X.

items.title: Anthropic launches Claude Design, hiring senior designers at $400K+
items.summary: Anthropic announces Claude Design and is hiring senior designers at $400K+ compensation. Signals a design-first product direction for Claude alongside the engineering-centric Claude Code push.

items.title: Google: Gemini expands in Chrome to more Asia-Pacific countries with summarization and image editing
items.summary: Google announced Gemini features in Chrome are expanding to more Asia-Pacific countries, adding page summarization, image editing, and broader integrations. The rollout extends Google's browser-native AI layer to a wider international audience as part of Chrome's AI-first roadmap.

items.title: Jeff Bezos' Project Prometheus nears $10B fundraising deal at $38B valuation
items.summary: Jeff Bezos' AI industrial venture Project Prometheus is nearing completion of a $10 billion fundraising round at a $38 billion post-money valuation, following an initial $6.2 billion raise in November 2025. The company focuses on applying AI to industrial applications, positioning it as a major new frontier AI player alongside established labs.

items.title: Anthropic board director Chris Liddell: NZ has 5 years — not 20 — to adapt to AI
items.summary: Chris Liddell, Anthropic board director and NEXT Foundation chairman, warned New Zealand it has 5 years — not 20 — to adapt or risk falling behind in the AI era. His comments during Microsoft's Auckland AI summit frame AI readiness as a national emergency.

items.title: How to Ground a Korean AI Agent in Real Demographics with Synthetic Personas
items.summary: Guide on building Korean AI agents using NVIDIA Nemotron with synthetic personas to ensure demographic accuracy and cultural relevance in AI systems.

items.title: Moonshot Kimi K2.6: world's leading Open Model refreshes to catch up to Opus 4.6
items.summary: Moonshot released Kimi K2.6, a 1T-parameter MoE open model with 32B active parameters, achieving strong performance on coding and agent benchmarks. Capabilities in long-horizon execution and multi-agent coordination.

items.title: AI and the Future of Cybersecurity: Why Openness Matters
items.summary: HuggingFace argues that open AI models are essential for cybersecurity: open weights let defenders audit, adapt, and harden AI systems without vendor lock-in, while closed models create opaque attack surfaces. The post surveys open-source security tooling built on HF-hosted models and calls for the community to prioritise security benchmarks alongside capability evals.

items.title: Scaling Codex to enterprises worldwide
items.summary: OpenAI launches Codex Labs for enterprise, partnering with Accenture, PwC, and Infosys to deploy Codex across software development lifecycles at scale. The rollout comes as Codex reached 4 million weekly active users in under two weeks, validating rapid adoption of AI-native coding agents beyond individual developers.

items.title: Microsoft doubles AI upskilling commitment in NZ, targeting 200,000 Kiwis by 2028
items.summary: Microsoft President Jane Livesey announced NZ$9.4 billion in economic contribution to New Zealand in FY25 and a commitment to double AI and digital upskilling initiatives, reaching 200,000 Kiwis by 2028. The report highlights the NZ North datacentre region's role in enabling digital sovereignty alongside Microsoft's AI partnership commitments.

items.title: Microsoft CEO in Auckland: AI could add $102B to NZ economy by 2038
items.summary: Microsoft CEO Satya Nadella visited Auckland and projected AI could add NZ$102 billion to New Zealand's economy by 2038. Microsoft also announced expanded AI training initiatives alongside the visit, framing AI adoption as urgent for NZ productivity and competitiveness.

items.title: NZ Law Commission launches review of AI in public sector decision-making
items.summary: New Zealand's Law Commission is launching a formal review of AI use in public sector decision-making, responding to legal challenges around AI-driven government outcomes. The review will examine accountability, transparency, and citizen appeal rights for automated decisions.

items.title: NZ startup Ideally raises $16M at $100M valuation for AI market research platform
items.summary: Auckland-based Ideally raised NZ$16M at a NZ$100M valuation for its AI-powered market research platform. The tool automates consumer insights gathering for enterprise clients, compressing research cycles from weeks to hours.

items.title: Christchurch-developed AI tool improves wildfire prediction accuracy by 30%
items.summary: A machine learning forecasting tool developed in Christchurch achieved 30% improvement in wildfire prediction accuracy using historical fire data from Australia. The model uses ensemble methods and is being evaluated for deployment across Pacific fire-prone regions.

items.title: Canva CEO on building proprietary AI, token pricing, and competing with AI-first design tools
items.summary: Canva CEO Melanie Perkins discusses the company's enterprise expansion, its investment in proprietary AI models, and how token-based pricing is reshaping competitive dynamics in the design software market. She addresses Canva's positioning against Adobe's AI suite and the growing wave of AI-first design entrants.

items.title: Maximizing Memory Efficiency to Run Bigger Models on NVIDIA Jetson
items.summary: Technical guide covering quantization, flash attention, and KV cache management techniques to deploy multi-billion-parameter generative AI models on memory-constrained NVIDIA Jetson edge devices.

items.title: Run High-Throughput Reinforcement Learning Training with End-to-End FP8 Precision
items.summary: NVIDIA details end-to-end FP8 precision training for LLM reinforcement learning using GRPO, addressing GPU utilization bottlenecks during high-intensity RL phases with significant throughput improvements.

items.title: Karpathy: Bryan Johnson uses Claude to analyze 1.5 billion personal health data points
items.summary: Andrej Karpathy highlights Bryan Johnson's deep-dive into personal health analytics using Claude across 1.5 billion data points. Demonstrates frontier use of LLMs for personal longitudinal data reasoning at extreme scale.

items.title: AI told NZ tenant to claim $40,000; Tenancy Tribunal awarded $80
items.summary: A New Zealand tenant relied on an AI assistant to calculate her rental bond dispute — the AI suggested a $40,000 claim; the Tenancy Tribunal awarded $80. The case is circulating as a cautionary example of LLM hallucination risk in legal contexts.

items.title: Leaked deck reveals OpenAI ad partner selling ChatGPT placements by prompt relevance
items.summary: StackAdapt, an OpenAI advertising partner, is selling ChatGPT ad placements targeted by user query relevance according to a leaked internal deck. This marks a new monetization phase for LLM chat interfaces through contextual advertising.

items.title: OpenAI rolls out Chronicle for Codex on macOS
items.summary: OpenAI introduced Chronicle, a feature that builds memories from screen captures to enhance Codex's contextual awareness. Currently a research preview for Pro subscribers on macOS.

items.title: Amazon invests $25B more in Anthropic, total now $33B
items.summary: Amazon committed an additional $25 billion investment in Anthropic, bringing total investment to $33 billion. Anthropic pledged to spend over $100 billion on AWS infrastructure over the next decade.

items.title: Microsoft adjusts GitHub Copilot plans and pricing — Opus 4.7 restricted to Pro+
items.summary: Microsoft paused new signups for GitHub Copilot Pro, Pro+, and Student tiers while tightening usage limits. Opus models removed from Pro and Opus 4.7 restricted to Pro+ tier only.

items.title: AI Resistance is growing: anti-AI pushback worth tracking
items.summary: Roundup of organized resistance to AI deployments across creative industries, academic institutions, and labor movements, with 150+ HN points. Covers key campaigns, boycotts, and legislation attempts targeting AI systems.

items.title: Microsoft plans token-based billing for GitHub Copilot
items.summary: Internal documents reveal Microsoft intends to shift GitHub Copilot from request-based to token-based billing. The week-over-week cost to run it has nearly doubled since January.

items.title: Reading today's open-closed performance gap
items.summary: Examines how the performance gap between open and closed AI models is misunderstood when reduced to a single benchmark score, exploring how benchmark evolution, model capabilities, and training regimes interact over time.

items.title: Mitigating Indirect AGENTS.md Injection Attacks in Agentic Environments
items.summary: NVIDIA details how AGENTS.md files can be weaponised for indirect prompt injection in multi-agent pipelines, and provides practical mitigations. Essential reading for anyone building or auditing production agentic systems.

items.title: Training Transformers to solve 95% failure rate of Cancer Trials — Noetik
items.summary: Noetik uses transformers trained on spatial transcriptomics to predict patient responses to cancer treatments. Their TARIO-2 model addresses a core bottleneck where 95% of cancer drugs fail clinical trials despite being effective for specific patient populations.

items.title: Deezer says 44% of songs uploaded to its platform daily are AI-generated
items.summary: The music streaming service reports that nearly half of daily song uploads are machine-generated, reflecting widespread adoption in music production.

items.title: Chollet: AI tools shift jobs toward higher complexity rather than eliminating them
items.summary: François Chollet argues AI coding tools like LLMs shift jobs toward higher-complexity work rather than eliminating them, driven by competitive advantage dynamics — echoing Aaron Levie's view that AI raises the floor for what developers tackle.

items.title: Adobe Agents Unlock Breakthrough Creative Intelligence With NVIDIA and WPP
items.summary: NVIDIA, Adobe, and WPP collaborate on agentic AI for enterprise marketing. Adobe's creative platforms + WPP's expertise + NVIDIA's Nemotron models and OpenShell runtime enable intelligent systems that plan, create, produce and activate content continuously while maintaining brand safety.

items.title: Import AI 454: Automating alignment research; Huawei HiFloat4; Kimi K2.5 safety study
items.summary: Anthropic demonstrates autonomous AI agents conducting alignment research that outperform human researchers (97% recovery vs 23%). Also covers Huawei's HiFloat4 training format outperforming Western MXFP4, and safety evaluation of Chinese model Kimi K2.5 showing fewer CBRN refusals than Western counterparts.

items.title: Atlassian enables default data collection to train AI
items.summary: Atlassian activates automatic data gathering from users to support artificial intelligence model development.

items.title: LeCun: consult economists not AI CEOs on job displacement; AI adds ~0.6% GDP annually
items.summary: Yann LeCun pushes back on Hinton's AI job displacement warnings, arguing labour economists like Daron Acemoglu are better authorities on workforce impact than AI scientists or CEOs. LeCun estimates AI may add approximately 0.6% annual GDP growth rather than causing mass unemployment.

items.title: NVIDIA and Partners Showcase the Future of AI-Driven Manufacturing at Hannover Messe
items.summary: NVIDIA demonstrates AI-powered manufacturing innovations including factory digital twins, vision AI agents, and humanoid robots. Partners show how accelerated computing, AI physics, agents and robotics are powering industrial innovation.

items.title: Claude Token Counter, now with model comparisons
items.summary: Tool upgraded to compare token counts across Claude models. Opus 4.7 uses roughly 1.46x the number of tokens as Opus 4.6 on system prompts.

items.title: OpenAI helps Hyatt advance AI among colleagues
items.summary: Hyatt deploys ChatGPT Enterprise across its global workforce, utilizing GPT-5.4 and Codex to enhance productivity, operations, and guest experiences.

items.title: Headless everything for personal AI
items.summary: Analysis of emerging trend toward API-first services designed for AI agents rather than GUI-based user interfaces, reshaping SaaS pricing models.

items.title: Changes in the system prompt between Claude Opus 4.6 and 4.7
items.summary: Detailed analysis of system prompt modifications including expanded child safety instructions, improved tool search capabilities, and reduced verbosity guidance.

items.title: Claude system prompts as a git timeline
items.summary: Research project converting Anthropic's published system prompts into a git repository with fake commit dates to track changes across Claude model versions.

items.title: The Two Sides of OpenClaw
items.summary: Analysis contrasting OpenClaw's public narrative of breakthrough AI agents with engineering realities of unprecedented security incidents and scaling challenges as the fastest-growing open source project in history.

items.title: Anthropic Claude Opus 4.7 — literally one step better than 4.6 in every dimension
items.summary: Claude Opus 4.7 launched with improvements across coding, vision, and reasoning tasks. Supports images up to 3.75 megapixels (3x larger than prior versions), introduces a new xhigh reasoning effort level, and demonstrates approximately 11-point gains on SWE-Bench Pro.

items.title: Grok Speech-to-Text and Text-to-Speech APIs
items.summary: xAI launches STT and TTS APIs via Grok, opening voice-interface capability to developers. Expands xAI beyond text-only LLM into the multimodal audio stack.

items.title: Ecom-RLVE: Adaptive Verifiable Environments for E-Commerce Conversational Agents
items.summary: Introduces Ecom-RLVE, an adaptive verifiable environment framework designed for training and evaluating e-commerce conversational agents with real-world scenarios.

items.title: Transformers to MLX: efficient inference on Apple Silicon
items.summary: Discusses converting Transformers models to MLX format, enabling efficient inference on Apple Silicon devices.

items.title: Training and Finetuning Multimodal Embedding & Reranker Models with Sentence Transformers
items.summary: Comprehensive guide on training multimodal embedding and reranker models using Sentence Transformers library for improved semantic search and ranking tasks.

items.title: My bets on open models, mid-2026
items.summary: Details key beliefs about open model development including technical strength of open labs, focus on benchmark scores versus robustness, dominance in repetitive automation tasks, and funding challenges for Chinese open-weight companies.

items.title: Gemini 3.1 Flash TTS: the next generation of expressive AI speech
items.summary: Google DeepMind introduced a new audio model with granular audio tags that give precise control for directing AI-generated speech with enhanced expressiveness.

items.title: Rethinking AI TCO: Why Cost per Token Is the Only Metric That Matters
items.summary: NVIDIA argues enterprises should evaluate AI infrastructure on cost per token rather than compute cost or FLOPS per dollar. Blackwell delivers 35x lower cost per million tokens compared to Hopper.

items.title: Inside VAKRA: Reasoning, Tool Use, and Failure Modes of Agents
items.summary: In-depth analysis of the VAKRA benchmark examining how AI agents reason, utilize tools, and fail, providing insights into agent behavior and limitations.

items.title: Import AI 453: Breaking AI agents; MirrorCode; gradual disempowerment
items.summary: METR and Epoch's MirrorCode benchmark demonstrates AI systems can reverse-engineer complex software with thousands of lines of code. Google DeepMind outlines six genres of attacks against AI agents and necessary mitigations across technical, ecosystem, and legal frameworks.

items.title: Introducing Muse Spark: Scaling Towards Personal Superintelligence
items.summary: Meta introduces Muse Spark, a creative AI system framed as a step towards personal superintelligence, focused on scaling generative and reasoning capabilities for individual users.
